$comment: The built-in hard-question pack for the Model Interview (embedded into Semanticus.Engine like interview-fix-map.json — edit HERE, never a copy). Each template is a MODEL-AGNOSTIC question pattern distilled from the benchmark's trap families: the shapes of question an AI assistant most often answers confidently wrong. Templates carry NO data-specific text and NO gold values — HardQuestionPack.Bind instantiates one only when every shape in `needs` exists in the open model, and the trusted answer is confirmed by a human before add_interview_question records it as an oracle. Tokens: {measure} {measureRef} {factTable} {dim} {dimCol} {dateTable} {dateCol} {dateAttr} {dateAttrCol} {entity} {entityKeyCol} {num1} {num1Col} {num2} {num2Col} {inactiveFrom} {inactiveFromCol} {inactiveToCol} {inactiveToTable}. needs vocabulary: measure | dimension | dateTable | dateAttr | entityKey | twoNumeric | inactiveRel.

templates.question: Which {dim} values rank highest by {measure}, and how are ties handled?
templates.trap: Ranking with ties: skipped ranks and shared ranks silently disagree, so two plausible answers differ.
templates.query: EVALUATE TOPN(10, ADDCOLUMNS(ALLNOBLANKROW({dimCol}), "Rank", RANKX(ALLNOBLANKROW({dimCol}), CALCULATE({measureRef}),, DESC, Dense)), [Rank], ASC) ORDER BY [Rank] ASC

templates.question: What is the year-to-date {measure} for each {dateAttr}?
templates.trap: A year-to-date calculation wipes a filter on a text date attribute (like a weekday name) unless the slice is explicitly kept, so the split quietly reverts to the unsliced total.

templates.question: How many distinct {entity} were there in the rolling 12 months up to the latest date?
templates.trap: Distinct counts do not add up across months, so summing monthly distinct counts overstates the rolling figure. The window here anchors on the date table's last date — if your calendar runs past the data, confirm the anchor.
templates.query: EVALUATE VAR LastDay = MAX({dateCol}) RETURN ROW("Rolling 12-month distinct {entity}", CALCULATE(DISTINCTCOUNT({entityKeyCol}), DATESINPERIOD({dateCol}, LastDay, -12, MONTH)))

templates.question: What was {measure} in the previous full month?
templates.trap: "Last month" quietly becomes "same days last month" or a partial month; the honest answer covers the previous COMPLETE month.
templates.query: EVALUATE VAR LastDay = MAX({dateCol}) RETURN ROW("Previous full month {measure}", CALCULATE({measureRef}, DATESBETWEEN({dateCol}, DATE(YEAR(EOMONTH(LastDay, -1)), MONTH(EOMONTH(LastDay, -1)), 1), EOMONTH(LastDay, -1))))

templates.question: What share of total {measure} does each {dim} contribute?
templates.trap: "Percent of total" is ambiguous once anything is filtered: share of the filtered total and share of the grand total are different numbers, and the wrong denominator looks perfectly plausible.
templates.query: EVALUATE TOPN(10, ADDCOLUMNS(ALLNOBLANKROW({dimCol}), "Share of total", DIVIDE(CALCULATE({measureRef}), CALCULATE({measureRef}, REMOVEFILTERS({dimCol})))), [Share of total], DESC) ORDER BY [Share of total] DESC

templates.question: What is the closing {measure} at the latest date with data, rather than the sum over time?
templates.trap: Balance-style measures must not be summed across dates: the honest figure is the value on the last date that has data, not the total of every day.

templates.question: How does year-to-date {measure} compare with the same period last year?
templates.trap: Comparing a partial year against a FULL prior year flatters or hides the trend; the honest comparison is period-to-date against the same days last year. Anchored on the date table's last date — if your calendar runs past the data, confirm the anchor.
templates.query: EVALUATE ROW("Year to date {measure}", CALCULATE({measureRef}, DATESYTD({dateCol})), "Same period last year", CALCULATE({measureRef}, SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR(DATESYTD({dateCol}))))

templates.question: How many {entity} from last year are still active this year to date?
templates.trap: Retention needs the INTERSECTION of two periods' entity sets; counting either period alone, or double-counting, gives a confident wrong answer.
templates.query: EVALUATE VAR ThisPeriod = CALCULATETABLE(VALUES({entityKeyCol}), DATESYTD({dateCol})) VAR PriorPeriod = CALCULATETABLE(VALUES({entityKeyCol}), SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR(DATESYTD({dateCol}))) RETURN ROW("{entity} kept since last year", COUNTROWS(INTERSECT(ThisPeriod, PriorPeriod)), "{entity} last year", COUNTROWS(PriorPeriod))

templates.question: What is the average {num1} weighted by {num2}, rather than the simple average?
templates.trap: A simple average of rows and a weighted average are different numbers whenever the weights vary — and the simple one is what gets reached for by default. Review that {num1} weighted by {num2} makes business sense before trusting either number.
templates.query: EVALUATE ROW("Weighted average {num1}", DIVIDE(SUMX({factTable}, {num1Col} * {num2Col}), SUM({num2Col})), "Simple average {num1}", AVERAGE({num1Col}))

templates.question: What is the total {measure} when related through {inactiveFrom} instead of the default relationship?
templates.trap: With two date roles (ordered vs shipped, opened vs closed) only one relationship is active; totals by the other role need the inactive relationship activated, or the answer silently uses the wrong dates.
templates.query: EVALUATE ROW("{measure} by {inactiveFrom}", CALCULATE({measureRef}, USERELATIONSHIP({inactiveFromCol}, {inactiveToCol})), "{measure} by the default relationship", CALCULATE({measureRef}))

templates.question: How many {dim} values have no {measure} at all, and is that blank rather than zero?
templates.trap: A blank (no activity at all) and an exact zero are different facts; treating blank as zero miscounts both groups. If no values are blank the first figure itself comes back blank, not zero.
templates.query: EVALUATE ROW("{dim} values with no {measure} (blank)", COUNTROWS(FILTER(ALLNOBLANKROW({dimCol}), ISBLANK(CALCULATE({measureRef})))), "{dim} values at exactly zero", COUNTROWS(FILTER(ALLNOBLANKROW({dimCol}), CALCULATE({measureRef}) = 0)))

templates.question: Do the {dim}-level values of {measure} add up to the overall total?
templates.trap: For non-additive measures (ratios, distinct counts, balances) the parts do NOT sum to the total; adding up a breakdown is the classic confident wrong answer.
templates.query: EVALUATE ROW("Sum of {dim} parts", SUMX(ALLNOBLANKROW({dimCol}), CALCULATE({measureRef})), "Overall total", CALCULATE({measureRef}, REMOVEFILTERS()))
